evidence_rea: the transfer is based on our legitimate interests and the legal basis is Art 6 para 1 lit f) GDPR
evidence_reg: The Controller SHALL provide the data subject with all of the following information: (d) where the processing is based on point (f) of Article 6(1), the legitimate interests pursued by The Controller or by a third party
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA states that the transfer is based on legitimate interests under Art 6(1)(f), while the REG requires providing that information to the data subject; these are related but not clearly the same constraint, so no quote-supported mismatch is established.

evidence_rea: the transfer is based on our legitimate interests and the legal basis is Art 6 para 1 lit f) GDPR
evidence_reg: the controller shall provide the data subject with the following information necessary to ensure fair and transparent processing in respect of the data subject: (b) where the processing is based on point (f) of Article 6(1), the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA identifies legitimate interests as the legal basis, whereas the REG imposes an information-provision duty about such interests; the texts do not clearly conflict on the same obligation.

evidence_rea: the transfer is based on our legitimate interests and the legal basis is Art 6 para 1 lit f) GDPR
evidence_reg: processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party
Point (f) of the first subparagraph shall not apply to processing carried out by public authorities in the performance of their tasks.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA generally invokes Art 6(1)(f) legitimate interests for the transfer, and the REG sets conditions and an exception for applying that legal basis; the REA does not clearly contradict those conditions on its face.
